methodology.important_note: These scores are non-diagnostic heuristics based on the health profile, Apple Health-derived metrics, and the 2020 and 2026 blood-test summaries. They are intended for personal tracking and visualization, not medical diagnosis.

overall.label: good overall health with several high-performing systems, but meaningfully reduced by cardiovascular risk and recent substance/trauma burden

areas.label: Body Composition
areas.summary: Lean, healthy body composition for age with low waist circumference and no excess-weight signal.
watch_items: Weight data is sparse after Aug 2024

areas.label: Metabolic Health
areas.summary: One of the strongest domains. Glucose handling and insulin sensitivity are excellent.
positives: Lean physique supports low diabetes risk
watch_items: Irregular eating pattern is not ideal even with good current markers

areas.label: Cardiovascular Risk
areas.summary: Mixed picture: strong fitness and very low inflammation, but the lipid panel is the clearest biological weakness in the current profile.
watch_items: Chronic cocaine exposure likely worsened vascular risk meaningfully

areas.label: Cardiorespiratory Fitness
areas.summary: Clearly above average for age, with good activity levels, favorable heart-rate markers, and a solid VO2 max.
positives: VO2 max is strong for age
Walking heart-rate average improved in the recent window
watch_items: Current pattern appears less aerobic than earlier outdoor-heavy periods

areas.label: Sleep & Recovery
areas.summary: Sleep quantity is decent, but fragmentation and trauma-related stress wakeups reduce the score materially.
positives: Recent HRV trend is somewhat improved
watch_items: Frequent brief awakenings / fragmented sleep proxy
Bedtime is late and circadian timing may be unstable across locations

areas.label: Kidney Health
areas.summary: Mostly good, but not perfect: creatinine/GFR are strong, while Cystatin C suggests mild stress worth monitoring.
watch_items: Past cocaine/ketamine use plausibly contributed subtle kidney stress

areas.label: Liver & Pancreas
areas.summary: A strong area. Despite prior substance burden, the measured liver and pancreatic markers stayed clean.
watch_items: Still worth protecting given past substance exposure

areas.label: Blood, Immune & Inflammation
areas.summary: Very strong domain. CBC is stable and inflammation is exceptionally low.

areas.label: Hormonal & Thyroid
areas.summary: Overall strong endocrine picture with excellent thyroid function and testosterone, mildly held back by prolactin and SHBG.

areas.label: Micronutrients & Fatty Acids
areas.summary: Strong nutritional biomarker picture overall, with excellent omega-3 and vitamin D status.
positives: Magnesium, selenium, zinc are all good
watch_items: Vitamin B6 is mildly high from supplementation
Folate is normal but not especially high

areas.label: Lifestyle & Behavioral Risk
areas.summary: Strong on smoking, alcohol moderation, activity, and body composition; significantly reduced by the recent heavy cocaine/ketamine history and stress trauma burden.

areas.label: Mental & Emotional State
areas.summary: Currently improved and functioning, but recent history includes severe distress and trauma, so this area remains a real recovery domain rather than a strength domain.
watch_items: Ongoing recovery from abuse and high-intensity substance period

strengths: Liver and pancreas markers are strong
Body composition is lean and favorable
Omega-3 and vitamin D status are excellent
Activity level and fitness are above average for age
main_risks: Elevated LDL and triglycerides are the clearest current biological risk
Recent cocaine/ketamine history materially worsens cardiovascular and recovery picture
Sleep is adequate in duration but fragmented
Cystatin C suggests mild kidney stress worth rechecking
Recent trauma/stress history remains clinically relevant

priority_actions.action: Maintain complete cessation of cocaine and ketamine
priority_actions.expected_effect: largest improvement in cardiovascular risk, prolactin, kidney stress, sleep stability, and overall recovery score

details: continue emotional processing / therapy-style recovery work if available
monitor whether danger-type wakeups fully resolve

priority_actions.action: Retest bloodwork after a clean recovery period

age_context.summary: For a 32-year-old lean male, the broad picture is stronger than average overall. The main reason the overall score is not higher is not baseline health quality, but the combination of elevated lipids, recent substance-related risk, sleep fragmentation, and recent trauma burden.
